title: Extend kubectl with plugins
text:
This guide demonstrates how to install and write extensions for
kubectl
.
By thinking of core
kubectl
commands as essential building blocks for interacting with a Kubernetes cluster,
a cluster administrator can think of plugins as a means of utilizing these building blocks to create more complex behavior.
Plugins extend
kubectl
with new sub-commands, allowing for new and custom features not included in the main distribution of
kubectl
.
